facts.text: The Sanborn Area Historical Society, founded in 1978, dedicates itself to preserving and promoting the rich history of Sanborn and its surroundings.
Its museum houses a significant collection of historical artifacts, including farming tools, household items, clothing, and photographs.
The society hosts lectures, workshops, and historical reenactments throughout the year to educate the public about Sanborn’s past.
Sanborn Farm Museum offers visitors a glimpse into the region’s agricultural heritage with displays of historic farm equipment and life‑on‑the‑farm scenes.
Its close proximity to Niagara Falls makes Sanborn an ideal base for day trips to the world‑famous waterfalls.
Sanborn welcomes travelers with a relaxed atmosphere and serves as a gateway to unforgettable experiences in upstate New York.
